Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

SpTU 2, 016 [House building rituals][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
Column i
(start of column missing)
o i 1'o 1'

ina? x [...]

(o i 1') ... [...] and .... After that [...].

o i 2'2'

u₃ x x ar-ki-⸢šu₂⸣ [...]

o i 3'3'

e-ma KA₂ DUG i-ḫe-⸢ep-pe⸣ [...] x [x x]

(o i 3') Wherever the door (of the newly built house is), he breaks a pot. [...] ... [...].

o i 4'4'

ZA₃ u GUB₃ ina ZI₃.DA e-ṣir ZA₃.DU₈ ina gišMAR URUDU i-ḫal-liš1

(o i 4') He draws with flour on the right and left. He scrapes off the door frame with a spade (made) of copper, and he smears (and) anoints Kulla's altar-base with an admixture of clay from the Apsu, which (is) the place of (its) extraction.

o i 5'5'

u bi-il-la-at IM ABZU ša₂ a-šar ni-si-iḫ-tu₄

o i 6'6'

šu-ba-at dKULLA i-se-er i-la-ap-pa-at

o i 7'7'

dKULLA ana E₂ ki-ma it-tar-du

(o i 7') When Kulla has gone down into the house, drawings are made with šigūšu-flour and ennēnu-flour.

o i 8'8'

ZI₃ ŠE.MUŠ₅ ZI₃ šeIN.NU.ḪU in-ne₂-eṣ-ṣir

o i 9'9'

KI dEN.KI-GA.KE₄ giŠUTUG in-na-an-du-u

(o i 9') A place for Ea and a reed hut are laid out.

o i 10'10'

NINDA NINDA.I₃.DE₂.AM₃ ana dIDIM dAMAR.UTU u dARA

(o i 10') (There is) food (consisting) of a mersu-cake for Ea, Marduk and Usmu, (as well as for) the god of the house, the goddess of the house (and) the protective spirit of the house.

o i 11'11'

DINGIR E₂e dINANA E₂ dLAMMA E₂

o i 12'12'

SISKUR₂ in-naq-qi₂ A-MEŠ in-na-aš₂-šu-u

(o i 12') An offering is presented. Water is raised.

o i 13'13'

de₂-a u dAMAR.UTU u₂-na-aḫ-ma

(o i 13') He appeases Ea and Marduk and the āšipu-priest speaks to Ea and Marduk as follows.

o i 14'14'

KA.PIRIG a-na dIDIM u dAMAR.UTU ki-a-am DU₁₁.GA

o i 15'15'

KI.UŠ KID₃.KID₃.DA-KAM

(o i 15') (That is) the sequence for the ritual procedures.

o i 16'16'

de₂-a u dAMAR.UTU be-el ANe u KIti₃

(o i 16') Ea and Marduk, lords of heaven and earth, you (are) the builders of city and house.

o i 17'17'

e-piš IRI u E₂ at-tu-nu-ma

o i 18'18'

ši-ma-a-ta ša₂-a-ma2

(o i 18') Determining destinies (and) drawing up plans (are) in your hands.

o i 19'19'

uṣ-ṣu-rat uṣ-ṣu-ru ša₂ qa-ti-ku-nu-ma

o i 20'20'

ši-ma-a-ta ba-la-ṭu at-tu-nu-ma ta-šim-mu3

(o i 20') It is you who determine the destinies of life.

o i 21'21'

uṣ-ṣu-rat ba-la-ṭu at-tu-nu tu-uṣ-ṣara

(o i 21') It is you who draw up the plans of life.

Column ii
(start of column missing)
o ii 1'o 1'

[...] ⸢ŠU?⸣

(o ii 1') [...] ....

o ii 2'2'

[... li]-⸢te⸣-ep-pu-šu

(o ii 2') May he (the house's owner) always do [...].

o ii 3'3'

[... li]-⸢iḫ⸣-te-ed-de

(o ii 3') May he always be joyful [...].

o ii 4'4'

[x x u-ṣa]-⸢am⸣-ma-ru lik-šu-du

(o ii 4') May he achieve [...] (which) he strives for.

o ii 5'5'

[x] ⸢ba⸣-ša₂-a-tu₄ li-te-ed-diš

(o ii 5') [...] the things that exist, may he always renew them.

o ii 6'6'

⸢KA⸣.INIM.MA ŠUD₃.DA-KAM

(o ii 6') (That is) the wording of the prayer.

o ii 7'7'

⸢d⸣KULLA i-te-eb-be ina E₂ uš-te-eṣ-ṣe

(o ii 7') Kulla arises. He is brought out from the house.

o ii 8'8'

2 GI.IZI.LA₂ ana IGI-šu₂

(o ii 8') Two torches (are) in front of him.

o ii 9'9'

ḫul-dub₂ e₃-ba-ra-ab MIN<(ḫul-dub₂)> e₃-ba-ra-ab i-ra-gu-mu

(o ii 9') They call out 'Depart, evilly-striking demon!', (and) ditto (again).

o ii 10'10'

2 NIG₂.NA? ša₂ ZI₃ IMGAGA!(AŠ₂) qar-nu lu-li-mu

(o ii 10') (There are) 2 censers (full) of emmer flour, deer horn, cress, nīnû-plant (and) sulphur; 2 date-palm buds; 2 ēru-trees; 2 live sheep; 2 scapegoats; 2 sacrificial goats; 2 tamarisks; 2 whips; 2 kušgugalû-drums; 2 nigkalagû-drums; barley offerings; (and) 2 offering vessels.

o ii 11'11'

ZA₃.ḪI.LIsar u₂KUR.RAsar PIŠ₁₀.dID₂

o ii 12'12'

2 gišPEŠ.GIŠIMMAR 2 gišMA.NU 2 UDU.TI.LA

o ii 13'13'

2 MAŠ₂.ḪUL.DUB₂.BA 2 MAŠ₂.GI.IZI.LA₂

o ii 14'14'

2 ŠINIG 2 kušUSAN₃ 2 KUŠ.GU₄.GAL

o ii 15'15'

2 uruduNIG₂.KALA.GA ŠE.BIR.BIR.DE₃e

o ii 16'16'

2 dugSILA₃.GAZ-MEŠ e₂-a igi-bi lu₂ nud-a zig₃-ga

(o ii 16') The shouters shout out 'At the front of the house, reclining man, arise!'.

o ii 17'17'

ša-su-u₂ i-ša₂-as-su₂

o ii 18'18'

ḫul-dub₂ e₃-ba-ra a-me₈-lu₄ ip-pa-al

(o ii 18') The man answers 'Depart, evilly-striking demon!'.

o ii 19'19'

ri-ki-is ḪUL.DUB₂ u₂-še-eṣ-ṣe

(o ii 19') He brings out the ritual assemblage for the evilly-striking demon.

o ii 20'20'

KI.UŠ ḪUL.DUB₂.BA-KAM

(o ii 20') (That is) the sequence for the evilly-striking demon.

o ii 21'21'

e₂-a igi-bi lu₂ nud-a zig₃-ga

(o ii 21') 'At the front of the house, reclining man, arise!',

o ii 22'22'

e₂-a igi-bi šu MA? e₂-a igi-bi-iš₃

(o ii 22') 'At the front of the house, ...', 'To the front of the house',

o ii 23'23'

diŋir ḫul a-ba-me-en udug ḫul gidim idim kur-ra

(o ii 23') 'Evil god, who are you?', 'Evil udug-demon (and) severe ghost of the land',

o ii 24'24'

a-ba-me-en lu₂ tukun-na

(o ii 24') 'Who are you, man, if',

o ii 25'25'

udug ḫul a-la₂ ḫul šu bar-ra-a-meš

(o ii 25') 'Evil udug-demon (and) evil ala-demon set free',

Reverse
Column i
r i 1r i 1

ud gal usu im mu-un-sug₂-ge-eš-a

(r i 1) 'When great storm, strength (and) rain step forward',

r i 22

niŋ₂-ḫulu-da niŋ₂-ḫulu-da dkulla bala ki ra-aḫ?

(r i 2) 'With the evil', 'With the evil', 'Kulla, crossing over (and) striking the earth',

r i 33

saŋ-ba saŋ-ba saŋ-ba saŋ-ba nun e₂-a nam-nun-na

(r i 3) 'Curse, curse', 'Curse, curse', (and) 'Prince of magnificence in the house'.

r i 44

14 KA.INIM.MA-BI

(r i 4) (These are) its 14 incantations.

r i 55

dKULLA TA E₂ ki-ma it-ta-ṣu-u

(r i 5) When Kulla has gone out from the house,

r i 66

KA₂ AN.AŠ.AM₃ 7 GIŠ.ḪUR in-ne₂-eṣ-⸢ṣir⸣

(r i 6) 7 drawings are made on the outer gate.

r i 77

7 dugSILA₃.GAZ-MEŠ uḫ-te-ep-pu-u

(r i 7) 7 offering vessels are shattered.

r i 88

7 gišPEŠ.GIŠIMMAR 7 gišMA.NU iz-za-qa-pu

(r i 8) 7 date-palm buds (and) 7 ēru-trees are planted.

r i 99

dKULLA ana GU₂ ID₂.DA qa₂-du ṣu-⸢de-e⸣-šu₂

(r i 9) They lead Kulla to the bank of a watercourse together with his provisions.

r i 1010

i-re-ed-de-u₂-[šu]

r i 1111

ina u₂-šal-li ID₂ u₂-še-eš-še-bu-⸢šu⸣

(r i 11) They sit him down in a meadow by the watercourse.

r i 1212

NINDA NINDA.I₃.DE₂.AM₃ ana dIDIM u dAMAR.UTU GARan

(r i 12) Food (consisting) of a mersu-cake is set in place for Ea and Marduk.

r i 1313

SISKUR₂ in-na-aq-qi₂ NINDA NE.MUR.RA

(r i 13) An offering is presented. (There are also) bread (baked) in charcoal (and) 2 twin-vessels which are filled with billatu-beer.

r i 1414

2 dugMAŠ.TAB.BA ša₂ DIDA ma?-la?-a

r i 1515

NIG₂.NA u₂-sa₃-ar-ra-aq

(r i 15) He scatters a censer.

r i 1616

ana IGI dIDIM u dAMAR.UTU lu₂MAŠ.MAŠ ana IGI ID₂ kam DU₁₁.⸢GA⸣

(r i 16) Before Ea and Marduk the mašmaššu-priest speaks as follows in front of the watercourse.

r i 1717

KI.UŠ KID₃.⸢KID₃⸣.[DA-KAM]

(r i 17) (That is) the sequence for the ritual procedures.

r i 1818

de₂-a u d[AMAR.UTU ...]

(r i 18) Ea and Marduk [...] and his provisions [...].

r i 1919

u ṣu₂-⸢de⸣-[e-šu ...]

r i 2020

ina pi-[ku-nu ...]

(r i 20) With your mouths [...] to the house that [...].

r i 2121

ana E₂ ⸢ša₂⸣ [...]

r i 2222

ši-mat [...]

(r i 22) [...] the destinies of [...].

r i 2323

⸢u₂⸣-[ṣu-rat ...]

(r i 23) [...] the plans of [...].

(rest of column missing)
Column ii
r ii 1r ii 1

lu₂MU₇.MU₇ ki-a-am DU₁₁.GA

(r ii 1) The āšipu-priest speaks as follows:

r ii 22

dKULLA na-as-ḫa-a-ta uk-<ku>-ša₂-a-ta ṭar₂-dat

(r ii 2) Kulla, you are torn out, you are driven off, you are sent away.

r ii 33

dKULLA ANe tum₃-mat KIti₃ tum₃-mat

(r ii 3) Kulla, you are adjured by heaven, you are adjured by the Underworld.

r ii 44

de₂-a u dAMAR.UTU tu-um-ma-a-ta

(r ii 4) You are adjured by Ea and Marduk.

r ii 55

ddu-ri₂ u dda-ri₂ tu-um-ma-a-ta

(r ii 5) You are adjured by Duri and Dari.

r ii 66

dlaḫ₃-ma u dla-ḫa-ma tu-um-ma-a-ta

(r ii 6) You are adjured by Lahmu and Lahamu.

r ii 77

dA.LA.LA u d<BE>.LI.LI tu-um-ma-a-ta

(r ii 7) You are adjured by Alalu and Belili.

r ii 88

DINGIR-MEŠ a-šib ANe tu-um-ma-a-ta

(r ii 8) You are adjured by the gods living in heaven.

r ii 99

DINGIR-MEŠ a-šib KIti₃ tu-um-ma-a-ta

(r ii 9) You are adjured by the gods living in the Underworld.

r ii 1010

DINGIR-MEŠ a-šib DU₆.KU₃.GA tu-um-ma-a-ta

(r ii 10) You are adjured by the gods living on the Duku.

r ii 1111

lu tan-na-as-si-iḫ lu ta-at-ta-al-lak4

(r ii 11) You should be torn out, you should go off.

r ii 1212

lu te-re-eq lu te-ne₂-es-se

(r ii 12) You should be distant, you should withdraw.

r ii 1313

lu ta-at-ta-aṣ-ṣi

(r ii 13) You should go away for good.

r ii 1414

⸢d⸣E₂.KUR₃? u dGA.AR u₂-tam-mi-ka

(r ii 14) I have adjured you by Ekur and Gar.

r ii 1515

⸢la⸣ ta-tu-ru

(r ii 15) Do not return.

r ii 1616

[KA].⸢PIRIG u lu₂ŠITIM⸣ pa-ni-šu₂-nu u₂-saḫ-ḫa-ru-ma

(r ii 16) The āšipu-priest and the builder turn their faces aside and they throw [...] of the left into the watercourse.

r ii 1717

[... 2].⸢30⸣ ana ID₂ i-na-as-su-ku

r ii 1818

[...] ⸢E₂⸣ BI la₃ KU₄

(r ii 18) He may not enter that house [...].

r ii 1919

[x x KID₃.KID₃].DA-KAM

(r ii 19) (That is) the [...] for the ritual procedures.

r ii 2020

[... IGI]-⸢šu₂⸣ GARan

(r ii 20) [...] are placed [...] front of him.

r ii 2121

[...] ⸢SIG₂⸣ GE₆

(r ii 21) [...], black wool, [...], yellow wool, [...] ...

r ii 2222

[...] ⸢SIG₂?⸣ SIG₇

r ii 2323

[...] x x GA

(rest of column missing)

1ihallaš expected rather than ihalliš

2šīmāta unexpected

3šīmāta unexpected; tašimmā expected rather than tašimmū

4tannassah expected rather than tannassih
